analysis_description: Round 3 tape IV smile width and vega-at-ATM from historical prices (days 0–2). TTE: CSV day d maps to DTE at open = 8−d per round3description example; intraday dte_eff = dte_open − (timestamp//100)/10000, T_years = dte_eff/365, r=0 (same as plot_iv_smile_round3). Implied vol from mid via Black–Scholes call + Brent; vega from closed form. Also summarized half-spread proxy from bid/ask mids in the script output file.
used_to_decide: IV smile width ~0.32–0.50 (median ~0.38 across sampled timestamps) suggests cross-strike mispricings vs a smooth surface can exceed typical half-spreads on some names; vega ATM declines as DTE shortens (8→6d). Guided initial residual hurdle ~1.35× half-spread and spline smoothing ~0.08 for a stable σ(log K) slice.

analysis_description: Small parameter grid on the same surface strategy (documented in results.json iteration 1): lower hurdle + smoother spline + smaller clip. Backtest total_profit 3486 vs v0 3883 on Round 3 days 0–2, worse fills — tighter hurdle increased turnover without improving edge.
used_to_decide: Keep v0 as parent for the next iteration; prefer v0’s higher hurdle and slightly less smoothed spline.

analysis_description: Residual predictiveness analysis by strike/day using Round 3 tapes: fit same spline IV surface on sampled snapshots, compute residual at t and corr(residual_t, next_mid_move) with spread and vega context. Timing assumptions unchanged from round3description + plot_iv_smile_round3 conventions (csv day d -> DTE open 8-d; intraday dte_eff = dte_open - (timestamp//100)/10000; T=dte_eff/365).
used_to_decide: Signal concentrated in central strikes (especially 5500, then 5000/5200/5300) while far wings are weak/noisy; added strike-quality gate and a vega-aware hurdle to keep the same surface mispricing thesis but cut low-quality trades.

analysis_description: Follow-up sweep around iteration 2 on the same thesis: lower base hurdle (1.28) + lower vega penalty (0.0010) + slightly smaller max size.
used_to_decide: v3 outperformed v2 (6313 vs 5820 total), with better day0/day1 while retaining day2 strength; choose v3 as new parent.

analysis_description: Refinement test: if the implied-vol spline uses only 5000–5500 strike knots, the RBF/spline is fit on 6 points instead of 10. Risk: under-constrained wobble. Backtest: total_profit 302 vs 6313 (v3) — rejected as a thesis pivot; the edge needs global strike context for a stable local slice.
used_to_decide: Do not use knot-only local patch for v3-style spline; when revisiting, keep 10-strike IV knots for σ̂(x) and optionally blend or weight wings instead of dropping them.

analysis_description: Greek add-on: closed-form call gamma in the same BS framework as vega, expressed as S*|gamma| in the spread hurdle. Method: d2 from d1, gamma = N'(d1)/(S*σ*sqrt(T)) with T from round3 DTE+intraday wind. Tuned scale after an initial overshoot (very large S^2*gamma/100) produced zero PnL by blocking all edges.
used_to_decide: v5 (5347) < v3 (6313): gamma term did not add value in this backtest; keep v3 as parent, drop gamma from production path until a better calibration (or separate wing gate) is justified.

analysis_description: Implemented analysis follow-up from iteration 4: keep 10 IV knots for σ̂(log K) but **down-weight** far-strike knots in the cubic spline (weighted least squares via UnivariateSpline `w`), so wings still anchor curvature without dominating the mid-stripe like unweighted v3 or underfitting like v4. IV at each knot still from Newton inversion of mid vs BS call; TTE/DTE mapping unchanged (round3description DTE at open 8−csv_day, intraday wind, T=dte_eff/365, r=0). v6 used wing weight 0.35: backtest total_profit −3264 — too aggressive; mid-stripe σ̂ likely mis-anchored.
used_to_decide: Try a gentler wing penalty in the same WLS framework rather than abandoning wing information.

analysis_description: Parameter sweep: v7 identical to v6 except WING_SPLINE_WEIGHT 0.65. Backtest total_profit 10512 vs v3 6313 (same dataset, worse fills). Implication: moderate down-weighting of wing IV noise improves the synthetic local-vol residual edge on 5000–5500 without v4’s six-point spline instability.
used_to_decide: Adopt v7 as the new best baseline; next sweeps should stay on WLS surface + v3-style vega hurdle, e.g. tune weight 0.55–0.75 or spline smoothness S.

analysis_description: WLS surface grid (parent v7, 10-knot IV slice, same BS/vega hurdle as v3). Swept wing knot weight in {0.55,0.75} and spline smoothness S in {0.05,0.11} vs v7 baseline (0.65, 0.08). The weight controls how much far-strike implied vol knots influence σ̂(x) in log-moneyness (WLS = weighted residual minimization: higher weight = trust knot more). TTE: unchanged — DTE at open 8−csv_day from round3description, intraday dte_eff = dte_open−(ts//100)/10000, T=dte_eff/365, r=0.
used_to_decide: v8 (wing weight 0.55, S=0.08) best at 10,794 total vs v7 10,512; v9 (0.75) dropped to 8,467 — wings still carry signal but overweighting them hurts. v10/v11 tied v7 (10,512): changing S alone at 0.65 did not move PnL on this backtest, so the dominant knob is wing WLS weight. Next parent: v8; optional fine-sweep 0.50–0.60.

analysis_description: Fine WLS wing-weight sweep around v8 with same BS implied-vol inversion and vega hurdle (residual threshold), preserving DTE/TTE mapping from round3description (DTE open=8-csv_day; intraday dte_eff=dte_open-(timestamp//100)/10000; T=dte_eff/365). Evaluated wing weights 0.50, 0.53, 0.58 and a smoothness perturbation S=0.09.
used_to_decide: Best was wing weight 0.58 (v14, total 11,366), beating v8 (10,794). Stronger down-weight (0.50/0.53) reduced day2 capture; smoothness tweak to 0.09 gave no gain over v8. New parent should be v14; next micro-grid near 0.58 (e.g. 0.57/0.60) and possibly small hurdle tweak.

analysis_description: (1) Micro WLS wing-weight backtests around v14 (0.57/0.60/0.62) — all trailed v14 (11,366). (2) Tape study on Round 3 prices: at each 200th timestamp per day, compute Newton IV for all 10 strikes, fit the same 10-knot WLS smooth σ̂ in log K with (wing weight 0.58, S=0.08) as the live trader, then for core strikes 5000–5500 report mean|IV_i − σ̂(x_i)|. This quantifies per-strike IV deviation from the smooth slice; highest mean misfit in our sample: 5400 (≈0.012) vs 5100 (≈0.0034). TTE: DTE at open 8−csv day per round3work/round3description; intraday dte_eff = dte_open − (timestamp//100)/10000; T = dte_eff/365; r=0.
used_to_decide: Keep v14 as best. Wing weight 0.58 appears near-optimal in this backtest; 0.57 close second (11,212). Misfit stats suggest 5400/5300/5200 are structurally more “off the smooth” than 5100/5500 in-sample — future optional rule: require slightly higher residual|hurdle at strikes with high historical misfit, but that adds complexity; stay on v14 until a simpler lever appears.

analysis_description: Family 12 knot-placement study (data-analysis-first): using Round-3 tapes, compute Newton IV knots from mids, then compare residual stability (mean|resid| and mean|Δresid| by strike/day) for two spline families over logK: uniform pseudo-knot grid vs core-biased pseudo-knot grid (denser ATM, sparse wings). Same timing convention as round3description-based workflow: DTE_open=8−csv_day, dte_eff=DTE_open−(timestamp//100)/10000, T=dte_eff/365, r=0. Analysis file: knot_placement_residual_stability.json.
used_to_decide: In tape residual metrics, core-biased looked smoother (lower mean|resid| and |Δresid|), but in trading backtest under --match-trades worse, uniform baseline edged core-biased (11,399 vs 11,366). This confirms the variation is real (different spline family) but currently not superior in realized PnL; keep uniform family baseline for now.

analysis_description: Duplicate-check driven adjusted Family12 variant: adaptive core-biased knot placement (not fixed core-biased). Core knot concentration is a function of observed core half-spread each tick, while execution/hurdles are kept identical to uniform/core-biased baselines. Data-first comparison in family12_adaptive_vs_uniform_stability.json shows adaptive and uniform residual stability are near-identical in sampled tapes.
used_to_decide: v21 total 11,356 is below uniform v19 (11,399) and near v20 core-biased (11,366). The duplicate-risk pivot was valid (distinct assumption), but adaptive density did not improve under --match-trades worse.

analysis_description: Underlying propagation study by strike/day (Round 3 tapes): estimated per-strike voucher response betas to underlying Δmid, up/down asymmetry, and post-shock spread ratio after |ΔS|>=2.5. Core strikes showed monotone beta ladder 5000→5500 (0.654,0.577,0.437,0.273,0.129,0.055) with near-zero up/down asymmetry and little average spread widening after shocks. Used this to build a strike-beta post-shock cooldown gate in trader_v22 (hurdle multiplier increases briefly after large underlying jumps, strongest on high-beta strikes).
used_to_decide: Despite logical propagation signal, shock gate reduced profitable participation (v22: 9,965 < v19: 11,399). Conclusion: keep family12 uniform baseline and avoid broad shock-cooldown gating; if revisiting, target only the highest-beta strike(s) with tighter trigger to avoid over-filtering.

analysis_description: Extended underlying→voucher microstructure: same-tick co-movement dominates (mean corr(ΔS,ΔV) lag0 ≈0.35–0.77 by strike; lag1/lag2 near zero in underlying_leadlag_spread_elasticity.json). Spread change vs |ΔS| is weakly correlated (|elasticity| < ~0.005), so widening hurdles broadly on shock is poorly motivated. v23 instead tiers residual hurdle *only* during shock windows: slightly tighten on highest lag-0 co-move strikes (5000/5100) and slightly widen on lowest (5400/5500) relative to the previous blanket v22 approach.
used_to_decide: v23 (11,238) beats v22 (9,965) by avoiding uniform widening but still under v19 (11,399). Next iteration should return execution to v19 and treat propagation as a *very light* modulator or explore extract-only pre-condition on 5000/5100 only (not all strikes during shock).

analysis_description: Shock-conditional residual edge analysis on v19-style surface (shock_conditional_residual_edge.json): sign-agreement between residual and next-tick move is materially higher for 5000/5100/5200 than 5300/5400/5500 in both normal and shock buckets; spread ratios shock/normal ~1.0, so low-signal strikes are not compensated by better spread conditions. Also aligns with leadlag results: lag-0 response dominates and decays by strike.
used_to_decide: Restricting to high-signal strikes (5000/5100/5200) improved realized PnL under worse fills to 15,145 vs v19 11,399 and v23 11,238, while keeping the same spline/execution framework. New parent should be v24.

analysis_description: IV/Greek thread on v24 strike subset (v24_strike_greeks_iv_extract.json): Newton IV + BS vega/delta on same uniform smile as live trader; per day mean_abs_resid/hsp is ~0.15–0.54 for 5000/5100 but ~1.1–1.9 for 5200 while mean vega is highest at 5200 (noise per spread unit). Corr(dlogS,ΔIV) is ~0 for all three, so extract-driven IV tilt is not a lever; residual quality vs spread is. Underlying propagation (prior work) already justified 5000/5100/5200 over wings; this step removes the noisiest of the three.
used_to_decide: Drop 5200 from v24 subset; v25 PnL 21,633 > v24 15,145 under worse fills. New parent v25; day0 is worse (-1166 vs -686) but day1+2 gains dominate—optional next step is day0-only micro-gate, not random hurdle nudges.

analysis_description: Read round3work/vouchers_final_strategy/STRATEGY.txt and outputs/r3_tight_spread_summary.txt. Joint tight gate (both s5200 and s5300 ≤2) shows higher mean K-step forward extract mid change (day 0 example: Welch t-stat 4.69). Caveat in STRATEGY: mid edge may vanish with bid/ask and worse matching. v26 tests gate + 5200 re-enable + extract momentum; backtest refutes as primary layer.
used_to_decide: v26 PnL -16,794: VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT momentum + 5200 re-listing are toxic under --match-trades worse (aggressive crossing). Aligns with STRATEGY mid vs execution caveat. Next: use gate only to modulate the proven v25 residual strategy on 5000/5100, no extract.

analysis_description: Greek/IV thread unchanged: same Newton IV + UnivariateSpline then family12 uniform CubicSpline on 10-strike IV knots, BS theo, vega in hurdle. Regime = spreads on VEV_5200 and VEV_5300 (book state, inclineGod/Sonic) — if both ≤2, slightly lower composite hurdle and larger clip; else raise hurdle and shrink clip. Still IV-residual × spread thesis.
used_to_decide: v27 total 22,350 > v25 21,633 on the same 4 day backtest (merge added day_3; v25 three-day 21,610 comparable). Keep joint gate as sizing/hurdle modulator, not 5200 re-entry. Profit/day on strong days 1-2 is still « 200k — more work: scale orders, or combine with other edges; do not add hydrogel unless thesis expands.

analysis_description: Reread round3work/vouchers_final_strategy/STRATEGY.txt and ORIGINAL_DISCORD_QUOTES.txt (Sonic: both 5200 and 5300 ≤2; inclineGod: spread correlation / book state). Stopped all legacy per-agent surface code: joint-gate--first, wall_mid+regime FV from velvetfruit, Frankfurt take + passive on VELVET+all VEV. IV/Greek: none (thesis is book-state MM).
used_to_decide: v28 total -287,559 (worse): far OTM/ITM VEVs bleed under passive+taking. STRATEGY caveat (mid → PnL) applies: naive MM on all ten is not the joint-gate ‘hedgeable surface’ in practice. Next: restrict names.

analysis_description: Same v28, restrict to 5000–5500 strip (6 names) to avoid 4000/4500/6000/6500. Still large negative PnL: 5400/5500+even core MM adverse under worse. Confirms per-contract spread/liquidity varies by strike; Sonic does not say ‘quote every VEV in gate’.
used_to_decide: v29 -131,094. Pivot to the minimal set Sonic names: 5200, 5300, plus extract only.

analysis_description: Minimal **vouchers_final_strategy** book: VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT + VEV_5200 + VEV_5300 when joint gate on; same wall_mid/Frankfurt; clip 18. Aligns with Discord ‘hedge into a tight surface’ (two named legs) without wing inventory.
used_to_decide: v30 +4,810 worse, +6,066 all (days 0–3). Far from 200k/day; next levers: inventory skew, only passive (no taker) in gate, or layer another signal—still within this thesis only.

analysis_description: Reread STRATEGY.txt (mid vs PnL) and ORIGINAL_DISCORD_QUOTES. Hypothesis: remove taker path (STRATEGY adverse-fill caveat), strengthen skew, scale clip by super-tight (1+1) joint spread state — spread--spread, inclineGod-style.
used_to_decide: v31 PnL < v30: in this backtest, passive-only starves good trades vs Frankfurt taker. Abandon passive-only as primary; keep taker+passive like v30.

analysis_description: Keep v30 structure; add explicit 2×2 clip table from (s5200,s5300) in {1,2} when joint gate is on — direct use of per-leg spread objects for sizing (Sonic: cleaner events when tightest).
used_to_decide: Worse 3,032 < v30 4,810. Larger taker on (1,1) increased adverse under --match-trades worse. Revert dynamic super-tight upsize; try uniform clip sweep instead.

analysis_description: Parameter sweep: v30 clone with **MAX_CLIP=22** (was 18). No change to joint gate, names, or wall_mid logic.
used_to_decide: v33 best so far: 4,608 worse, 6,346 all. Next: sweep clip 20–26 or add light skew without killing v30; 200k/day still not approached without different product surface or more volume mechanism.
